Title: Xan Irelia
Age: Current25 years old Timeline Born972 AN Edit

children.title: Early Life
children.textContent:
As a young girl, Xan Irelia was captivated by the grace and beauty of human movement. She learned the traditional silk dance of her province from her grandmother and later left home to study under Ionia's most respected dancers at the Placidium of Navori. When Noxus began its invasion, Irelia returned to find her village already occupied. Duqual, a Noxian admiral, had seized her family's home and executed her father and brothers for resisting.

As the Noxians looted the Xan household, Irelia tried to salvage her family's crest, but Duqual ordered his men to shatter it. He commanded them to dig a fresh grave for her, yet as they closed in, Irelia felt a strange rhythm begin to beat within her. The shards of the crest stirred, moving in time with her steps, and she felt the joy of the dance return. The fragments followed her movements, and with a sweep of her arm, they cut down the surrounding Noxians. Duqual and his soldiers recoiled in terror, allowing Irelia to escape with what remained of her family's legacy. Fleeing into the nearby forest, she mourned her loss. Reflecting on her grandmother's teachings, Irelia realized the dance was more than art, it was an expression of something greater.

As the invasion spread across the First Lands, scattered voices of dissent united into a resistance. Irelia joined them, performing her dance to preserve their culture. When she was fourteen, she and the resistance returned to the Placidium of Navori after it was captured by General Jericho Swain. Though his forces were spread thin, Swain had taken the Placidium to lure Ionia's defenders into an ambush.

There, Irelia rose to meet her destiny. Leading the charge with her blade dancing technique, she cut down many of Swain's veterans and struck down the general himself, severing his arm. Swain, however, survived.

News of her victory spread across Ionia, marking a turning point in the war and cementing the legend of Xan Irelia. Many looked to her for leadership, and though reluctant, she accepted the burden. For nearly three years, Irelia's resistance pushed Noxus back until the Battle of Dalu Bay, where she avenged her family by slaying Admiral Duqual. When Noxus retreated and the war ended, Ionia was forever changed and deeply divided. Though the people continued to look to her for guidance, Irelia longed only to dance alone once more.

children.title: Stains on a Name
children.textContent:
One of the factions that arose during the Noxian invasion was the Navori Brotherhood, a militant group that sought to unite Ionia under a single rule and was willing to do whatever it took to achieve that goal. Inspired by Irelia's actions, they wanted her to lead them, but she refused, believing their methods went against the teachings of the Great Spirit. In retaliation, they sent warriors to assassinate her, yet she repelled each attempt on her life.

After dispatching another assassin, Irelia returned to her tent and apologized to her father's spirit for staining her blades with the blood of her own people. As she cleaned them, she reflected on the teachings of Karma, who had once preached pacifism. Now, her family’s crest had become a weapon that had claimed countless lives. Irelia feared that her ancestors would be ashamed of what she had done, yet she still vowed to make amends for those she had harmed.

When she made her way to the burial grounds, many in the resistance still looked to her for guidance. However, with each passing winter, she recognized fewer and fewer faces, as more came from distant provinces to fight under her banner. She laid her latest attacker to rest among the fallen. Nearby, she heard a woman humming one of the old songs and joined her. Irelia confessed that she felt responsible for ending the peace in Ionia, but the woman replied that the world had changed, and what once worked no longer could. As the woman pried open a nut with a knife, she remarked that her younger self would have scolded her for doing so, but she was not the one living in the present. For the first time in a long while, Irelia smiled.

children.title: Rise of the Sentinels
children.textContent:
When the Ruination reached the First Lands, Irelia encountered the Sentinels of Light, initially believing they were responsible for the attack. She was reluctant to trust them, especially with a Noxian war criminal, Riven, among their ranks. However, Senna convinced her that there were greater threats at hand and that Riven now fought to protect Ionia—and the world—from the spreading darkness. Irelia explained that Ionia's spiritual safeguard, Karma, had been struggling since the Black Mist arrived and had been communing with the Spirit of Ionia in search of a solution. She guided the Sentinels to the Lasting Altar, though she insisted that Riven remain outside.

Within the temple, Karma revealed that Viego sought the fetter in her possession, a silver flower. She proposed taking it to the Grove of Ancients for protection, believing even the Black Mist could not breach its barrier. Before they could depart, the Lasting Altar was besieged by an army of ghouls. Irelia and the Sentinels held them off, while Karma fled toward the forest. However, Karma began to behave erratically.

As they made their way to the Grove, Karma's regret over failing to intervene during the Noxian invasion consumed her, and she ultimately succumbed to Viego's corruption. Though she fled with the fetter, the Sentinels briefly disrupted Viego's hold over her. In that fleeting moment of clarity, Karma revealed that the fetters were fragments of Viego's queen's soul. She handed them the silver flower and ordered them to reach the Grove before Viego's ghouls took her.

The Sentinels eventually reached the Grove of Ancients, but Viego was able to corrupt the land itself, allowing him to enter and claim the fetter. Witnessing the Spirit of Ionia defiled, Irelia resolved to help restore it and agreed to join the Sentinels of Light. She returned with them to their headquarters, where she donned a new uniform and took up relic stone blades.

From then on, Irelia journeyed alongside the Sentinels as they raced across Runeterra to prevent Viego from collecting the remaining fetters. Though she and Riven frequently clashed, they gradually developed mutual respect for one another. Irelia ultimately took part in the final battle against Viego and his forces on the Shadow Isles. After the Ruined King was sealed away once more, she returned her Sentinel gear and went home to Ionia, having developed an unlikely friendship with Riven.

mainContent.title: Appearance
mainContent.textContent: Irelia is a slender, pale-skinned woman with sea green eyes and long dark hair down to her hips. Her clothes are a mix of elegant robes and battle attire with a distinctive palette of red, pink, black, and silver. The most characteristic element of her attire is the silver diadem on her head. She also has several 1 silver blades that float around her.

mainContent.title: Personality
mainContent.textContent:
Irelia is a passionate, protective individual who dedicates herself to the protection of Ionia and is very fond of dance. She believes that brute force is needed to properly defend Ionia from the likes of Noxus, hence why she considers alliances with Zed and his shadow assassins despite their disagreeable methods. She also demonstrates a kind and caring persona to those close to her, such as the Blossoming Blade, whom she has a soft spot for.

Despite her uptight and serious demeanour, however, Irelia also displays a level of sarcasm at her opponents. This is evidenced by her comments on Garen's fighting technique and his habit of yelling his country's name in battle.

children.title: Swain
children.textContent: Irelia cut off Swain's arm which resulted in him contacting and taming a demon of secrets named Raum, later becoming the Grand General of Noxus. For this reason, he is somewhat grateful for her action.

children.title: Riven
children.textContent: Irelia was heavily antagonistic towards Riven due to her past actions during the Noxian Invasion, but grew to respect her during their time together as Sentinels of Light.

children.title: Sona
children.textContent: Sona's in-game voice lines suggest she and Irelia met at some point when she returned to Ionia.

children.title: Ionians
children.textContent:
The Ionian resistance led by Irelia in the war later sprung into different groups with different ideologies, including the Navori Brotherhood and the Navovi Militia. The Brotherhood, in particular, became more aggressive when she wasn't the hardcore leader they needed and has tried to kill her multiple times.

Irelia dislikes those with more pacifist attitudes like those of Karma and Ivern, or those who do not protect their homeland such as the Kinkou Order since she believes this would only lead to the fall of Ionia. She may also be willing to side with Ionian extremists like Zed's Order of Shadow, of even the Navori Brotherhood if necessary despite not approving their methods.

children.title: Liana
children.textContent: As told in Legends of Runeterra, her resistance force includes peasants of Ionia, musicians and dancers. Among those, Irelia appears to have the most intimate relationship with 5 Liana.

mainContent.textContent:
